context: Tests whether the agent correctly selects commit types, uses imperative mood, keeps headers concise, avoids chore as a catch-all, and follows basic header formatting rules across a batch of commit message corrections.

checklist.name: feat for new feature
checklist.description: The corrected message for the new export endpoint uses 'feat' type (not 'chore', 'build', or another type)

checklist.name: fix for bug correction
checklist.description: The corrected message for the null pointer crash uses 'fix' type (not 'chore')

checklist.name: test for test addition
checklist.description: The corrected message for the test coverage update uses 'test' type

checklist.name: ci for pipeline update
checklist.description: The corrected message for the CI pipeline changes uses 'ci' type

checklist.name: refactor for restructuring
checklist.description: The corrected message for the authentication module restructuring uses 'refactor' type

checklist.name: Imperative mood
checklist.description: All corrected headers use imperative mood (e.g. 'add', 'fix', 'extract') rather than past tense ('added', 'fixed', 'updated')

checklist.name: No trailing period
checklist.description: None of the corrected headers end with a period

checklist.name: Header ≤ 72 chars
checklist.description: All corrected headers (type + scope + description) are 72 characters or fewer

checklist.name: Lowercase description
checklist.description: All corrected description portions are lowercase (the text after the colon and space)

checklist.name: No chore misuse flagged
checklist.description: The explanation for message #2 identifies that 'chore' was used incorrectly (it was a bug fix, not maintenance)
